serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Golden Coin Eggs is approximately 150 g, typically comprising 2–3 fried egg rounds stir-fried with chilies, garlic, ginger, and fermented black beans, served as a side dish alongside steamed rice.

nutrient_source.title: Egg, whole, cooked, fried — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 173424)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No approved-source entry exists for Golden Coin Eggs as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for fried whole eggs (FDC ID 173424) as the primary base, adjusted upward for sodium contribution from fermented black beans (douchi) and soy sauce, and adjusted for vitamin C and dietary fibre from fresh chili peppers. The input AI-generated values (confidence 0.9) were cross-checked against this USDA base and found to be broadly consistent. Values retained with source reclassified as adapted/estimated from USDA SR Legacy. Saturated fat estimated at approximately 3.5 g/100 g based on USDA fried egg lipid profile. The original input cited Hunan Agricultural University (hunau.edu.cn) as a nutrition source; this is not an approved source under Project Veritas v2 and has been superseded by USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index — not applicable for egg-dominant dish with negligible carbohydrate impact; reference: University of Sydney GI Database consulted, no applicable entry found
gi_source.source_match_notes: Golden Coin Eggs is an egg-dominant dish. Although a small amount of wheat flour is used for dusting and chilies contribute minor carbohydrates (total carbohydrate ~6.5 g/100 g), the dish is protein- and fat-dominant. The University of Sydney GI Database was consulted; no direct entry for Golden Coin Eggs or a sufficiently close Hunan egg dish was found. Per the Veritas critical rule for egg-based foods, glycemic_index is set to null and gi_category to not_applicable. The input record's estimated GI of 25 from Hunan Agricultural University (a non-approved source) has been superseded and discarded.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from fermented black beans (douchi) and soy sauce (~380 mg sodium per 100 g; ~570 mg per 150 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one small serving and request reduced soy sauce and fermented black bean seasoning, as the sodium content of approximately 570 mg per standard 150 g serving represents roughly 25% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low net carbohydrate content (~6.5 g/100 g) and high protein and fat content support glycemic stability
type2_diabetes.guidance: Golden Coin Eggs is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed in moderate portions, as the high protein (9.5 g/100 g) and fat (13.5 g/100 g) content relative to carbohydrates (6.5 g/100 g) supports slow glucose absorption; however, sodium from fermented black beans should be monitored in those with concurrent hypertension.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~3.5 g/100 g) and dietary cholesterol (~210 mg/100 g) from eggs, combined with elevated sodium from fermented black beans
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume Golden Coin Eggs in moderation; the dish provides approximately 210 mg dietary cholesterol and 3.5 g saturated fat per 100 g, and the sodium content of ~380 mg/100 g warrants attention in those managing blood pressure as a cardiovascular risk factor.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory compounds from ginger, garlic, and capsaicin in chilies; nightshade sensitivity possible
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Ginger and garlic in this dish have documented anti-inflammatory properties; however, individuals with rheumatoid arthritis who are sensitive to nightshades (Solanaceae family) should monitor their response to the chili pepper component.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Golden Coin Eggs (金钱蛋, Jīnqián dàn) is a classic home-style dish from Hunan Province (Xiang cuisine), China, named for the resemblance of its fried egg rounds to ancient Chinese gold coins. The preparation begins with hard-boiled eggs that are sliced into thick rounds, lightly dusted with wheat flour, and pan-fried in oil until the exterior becomes golden-brown and slightly crispy. The fried egg slices are then stir-fried with a generous quantity of fresh red and green chilies, garlic, ginger, and fermented black beans (douchi), producing a dish with bold, spicy, and savory umami flavors. The egg yolk remains creamy while the white develops a firm, lightly crisped texture that absorbs the intense aromatics of the Hunan seasoning. Nutritionally, per 100 g the dish provides approximately 185 kcal, 9.5 g protein, 13.5 g fat (including ~3.5 g saturated fat), and 6.5 g carbohydrate, with a notable sodium content of ~380 mg from fermented black beans and soy sauce. It is a good source of vitamin B12 (0.8 mcg), riboflavin (0.35 mg), selenium (22.5 mcg), and vitamin A (110 mcg RAE). Clinically, the dish is low in carbohydrates and suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes in moderate portions, but its sodium and cholesterol content warrant caution for those with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. It is served as a side dish at lunch or dinner alongside steamed rice.
common_user_queries: Hunan egg dish with chilies and fermented black beans
Is Golden Coin Eggs suitable for diabetics?

confidence_reason: No approved primary source (USDA FoodData Central, HPB Singapore, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia, FAO/INFOODS SEA) contains a direct entry for Golden Coin Eggs as a composite dish. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for fried whole eggs (FDC ID 173424) as the primary base, with adjustments for sodium from fermented black beans and vitamin C from chili peppers. The AI-generated input values (confidence 0.9) were cross-checked against this USDA base and found broadly consistent. Confidence is set to medium reflecting the adapted/estimated nature of the composite dish values.
notes: 1. NUTRIENT SOURCE: No exact match for Golden Coin Eggs exists in any approved primary source. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (fried whole egg, FDC ID 173424) as the primary base, with composite adjustments for fermented black bean sodium contribution and chili pepper vitamin C and fibre. This is documented as source_match_type: adapted. The original input cited Hunan Agricultural University (hunau.edu.cn) as a nutrition and GI source; this is not an approved source under Project Veritas v2 and has been fully superseded. 2. SATURATED FAT: saturated_fat_g estimated at 3.5 g/100 g based on USDA SR Legacy lipid profile for fried eggs; monounsaturated_fat_g estimated at 5.2 g/100 g and polyunsaturated_fat_g at 2.1 g/100 g on the same basis. trans_fat_g set to 0.0 as no trans fats are expected from this preparation. 3. CHOLESTEROL: cholesterol_mg estimated at 210 mg/100 g based on USDA SR Legacy data for fried whole eggs; this is plausible for an egg-dominant dish. 4. WATER: water_g set to -1 as moisture data for this composite Hunan preparation is not available in USDA FoodData Central or any other approved source as of 2026-04-17. 5. GLYCEMIC INDEX: The input record assigned a GI of 25 from Hunan Agricultural University, which is not an approved GI source under Project Veritas v2. The University of Sydney GI Database was consulted and contains no applicable entry for this egg-dominant dish. Per the Veritas critical rule for egg-based foods, glycemic_index is set to null (not 0) and gi_category to not_applicable. glycemic_load_per_serving is set to null accordingly. 6. PORTIONS: The input record contained a duplicate portion entry (two identical 150 g entries); deduplicated to a single 150 g default serving. 7. ONTOLOGY: FoodOn FOODON_03315595 (cooked egg food product) and FoodEx2 A00GJ (cooked egg dishes) used as approximate anchors with match_type closest_parent; no exact codes exist for this specific Hunan preparation. SNOMED CT left null — no applicable code identified. 8. ALIASES CONSISTENCY CHECK: All strings in aliases_by_language verified to appear verbatim in flat aliases array: Golden Coin Eggs ✓, Hunan Spicy Fried Eggs ✓, Hunan Gold Coin Eggs ✓, 金钱蛋 ✓, 金錢蛋 ✓, Jīnqián dàn ✓, Gam1 cin4 daan2 ✓, Jin Qian Dan ✓, Jin Qian Dan Hunan style ✓. Consistent.
